
```{r}
library("rpaleoclim")
library("here")
library("tidyverse")
library(terra)
library(sf)
source("general_functions.R")
ca <- get_ca()

# current
paleoclim("cur", "2_5m", region = ext(ca), cache_path = here("data", "env"))
# last glacial maximum
paleoclim("lgm", "2_5m", region = ext(ca), cache_path = here("data", "env"))
# late holocene
paleoclim("lh", "2_5m", region = ext(ca), cache_path = here("data", "env"))
# mid holocene
paleoclim("mh", "2_5m", region = ext(ca), cache_path = here("data", "env"))
# early holocene
paleoclim("eh", "2_5m", region = ext(ca), cache_path = here("data", "env"))

lgm <- load_paleoclim(here("data", "env", "chelsa_LGM_v1_2B_r2_5m.zip"))
lgm <- crop(lgm, ca)
lgm <- mask(lgm, ca)

cur <- load_paleoclim(here("data", "env", "CHELSA_cur_V1_2B_r2_5m.zip"))
cur <- crop(cur, ca)
cur <- mask(cur, ca)

lh <- load_paleoclim(here("data", "env", "LH_v1_2_5m.zip"))
lh <- crop(lh, ca)
lh <- mask(lh, ca)

mh <- load_paleoclim(here("data", "env", "MH_v1_2_5m.zip"))
mh <- crop(mh, ca)
mh <- mask(mh, ca)

eh <- load_paleoclim(here("data", "env", "EH_v1_2_5m.zip"))
eh <- crop(eh, ca)
eh <- mask(eh, ca)


par(mfrow = c(2,3))
library("RColorBrewer")

# Create a divergent color palette using the RdBu color scheme
palette <- rev(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))

plot(lgm[[1]])
plot(cur[[1]])
plot(cur[[1]] - lgm[[1]], col = palette)

plot(lh[[1]])
plot(cur[[1]])
plot(cur[[1]] - lh[[1]], col = palette)

par(mfrow = c(1,2))
plot(cur[["bio_1"]] - lh[["bio_1"]], col = palette)
plot(cur[["bio_1"]] - lgm[["bio_1"]], col = palette)

par(mfrow = c(1,2))
plot(cur - lh, col = palette)
plot(cur[["bio_5"]] - lgm[["bio_5"]], col = palette)

coords <- get_coords()
env <- terra::extract(cur, coords[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)

# # Load required libraries
library(biomod2)

# Load your environmental data
env_data <- cur
species_data <- data.frame(coords[,c("x", "y")], presence = 1)
pseudo_absence <- spatSample(env_data, 100, xy = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)
pseudo_absence$presence <- 0
species_data <- bind_rows(species_data, pseudo_absence) %>% select(x, y, presence) 

plot(cur[[1]])
points(species_data[,c("x", "y")], col = species_data$presence + 1)
# Prepare the data for biomod2
myBiomodData <- BIOMOD_FormatingData(resp.var = species_data$presence,
                                     expl.var = env_data,
                                     resp.xy = species_data[, c('x', 'y')],
                                     resp.name = 'Sceloporus')

# Define the models you want to run

# Define the models you want to run
myBiomodModelOut <- BIOMOD_Modeling(myBiomodData,
                                    models = 'RF',
                                    modeling.id = 'test',
                                    CV.perc = 0.9,
                                    CV.nb.rep = 10)

myProjection <- BIOMOD_Projection(bm.mod = myBiomodModelOut,
                                  new.env = env_data,
                                  proj.name = 'MyProjection',
                                  selected.models = 'all')    

LH <- BIOMOD_Projection(bm.mod = myBiomodModelOut,
                                  new.env = lgm,
                                  proj.name = 'LH',
                                  selected.models = 'all')                                    

cur_sdm <- mean(get_predictions(myProjection))/1000
lh_sdm <- mean(get_predictions(LH))/1000
plot(cur_sdm)
plot(lh_sdm)
plot(cur_sdm - lh_sdm, col = palette)

source(here("analysis", "genetic_diversity", "genetic_diversity.R"))
het <- get_het()
coords <- left_join(coords, het)
dif_lgm <- cur - lgm
dif_vals <- extract(dif_lgm, coords[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)
mod_df <- data.frame(Ho = coords$Ho, dif_vals)
pc <- prcomp(mod_df[,-1], scale = TRUE)

res <- summary(lm(Ho ~ ., data = mod_df))
sigvars <- names(which(res$coefficients[,"Pr(>|t|)"] < 0.05))[-1]

gg_df <- mod_df %>% pivot_longer(all_of(sigvars))
ggplot(gg_df) + 
  geom_point(aes(x = value, y = Ho)) + 
  geom_smooth(aes(x = value, y = Ho), method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free")

gg_df <- mod_df %>% pivot_longer(all_of("bio_9"))
ggplot(gg_df) + 
  geom_point(aes(x = value, y = Ho)) + 
  geom_smooth(aes(x = value, y = Ho), method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free")
plot(dif_lgm[["bio_9"]])

pcs <- data.frame(pc$x[,1:3])
pcmod_df <- data.frame(Ho = coords$Ho, pcs)
res <- summary(lm(Ho ~ ., data = pcmod_df))
sigvars <- names(which(res$coefficients[,"Pr(>|t|)"] < 0.05))[-1]
gg_df <- pcmod_df %>% pivot_longer(all_of(sigvars))
ggplot(gg_df) + 
  geom_point(aes(x = value, y = Ho)) + 
  geom_smooth(aes(x = value, y = Ho), method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free")
#sdm <- sdm(coordinates =  coords[,c("x", "y")], envlayers = stack(lgm), models = "RF", nbkg = 1000, tune = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# Calculate the difference between cur and lh
dif_lh <- cur - lh
dif_vals <- extract(dif_lh, coords[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)
mod_df <- data.frame(Ho = coords$Ho, dif_vals)
pc <- prcomp(mod_df[,-1], scale = TRUE)

# Perform linear regression and identify significant variables
res <- summary(lm(Ho ~ ., data = mod_df))
sigvars <- names(which(res$coefficients[,"Pr(>|t|)"] < 0.05))[-1]

# Create a long format dataframe for plotting
gg_df <- mod_df %>% pivot_longer(all_of(sigvars))

# Plot scatterplot with regression line and correlation coefficient
ggscatter(
  gg_df, x = "value", y = "Ho",
  add = "reg.line"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free") +
  stat_cor(label.y = 800000) 

# Check for correlations between variables
cmat <- cor(mod_df)
which(abs(cmat[,"bio_2"]) > 0.5)

# Create a long format dataframe for plotting
gg_df <- mod_df %>% pivot_longer(all_of(c("bio_2", "bio_5", "bio_7", "bio_6", "bio_12", "bio_13")))

# Plot scatterplot with regression line and correlation coefficient
ggscatter(
  gg_df, x = "value", y = "Ho",
  add = "reg.line"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free") +
  stat_cor(label.y = 800000) 

plot(dif_lgm[["bio_2"]])

# Extract loadings for PC1
loadings <- pc$rotation[, 1]
sorted_loadings <- sort(abs(loadings), decreasing = TRUE)
high_contrib_vars <- names(sorted_loadings)

# Print or explore high_contrib_vars
print(high_contrib_vars)

# Create a long format dataframe for plotting
gg_df <- pcmod_df %>% pivot_longer(all_of(sigvars))

# Plot scatterplot with regression line
ggplot(gg_df) + 
  geom_point(aes(x = value, y = Ho)) + 
  geom_smooth(aes(x = value, y = Ho), method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free")

# Create a long format dataframe for plotting
gg_df <- mod_df %>% pivot_longer(all_of(high_contrib_vars))

# Plot scatterplot with regression line
ggplot(gg_df) + 
  geom_point(aes(x = value, y = Ho)) + 
  geom_smooth(aes(x = value, y = Ho), method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free")

# Create a long format dataframe for plotting
gg_df <- mod_df %>% pivot_longer(all_of("bio_2"))

# Plot scatterplot with regression line
ggplot(gg_df) + 
  geom_point(aes(x = value, y = Ho)) + 
  geom_smooth(aes(x = value, y = Ho), method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free")

plot(dif_lh[["bio_2"]])

# Create a dataframe for plotting
bio_2 <- data.frame(cur = extract(cur[["bio_2"]], coords[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE),
                    lh = extract(lh[["bio_2"]], coords[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE),
                    dif = extract(dif_lh[["bio_2"]], coords[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE),
                    Ho = coords$Ho)
names(bio_2) <- c("cur", "lh", "dif", "Ho")
bio_2 <- bio_2 %>% pivot_longer(all_of(c("cur", "lh", "dif"))) %>% mutate(name = factor(name, levels = c("cur", "lh", "dif")))

# Plot scatterplot with regression line for each variable
ggplot(bio_2) + 
  geom_point(aes(x = value, y = Ho, color = name)) + 
  geom_smooth(aes(x = value, y = Ho, color = name), method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free") +
  theme_classic()

par(mfrow = c(1,3))
plot(lh[["bio_2"]], col = magma(100), range = c(0, 150))
plot(cur[["bio_2"]], col = magma(100), range = c(0, 150))
plot(dif_lh[["bio_2"]], col = magma(100))
```

```{r}
roh <- get_roh()
coords <- left_join(coords, roh)
cur_vals <- extract(cur, coords[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)
roh_mod_df <- data.frame(froh = coords$froh, cur_vals)

ggdf <- roh_mod_df %>% pivot_longer("bio_2")
ggplot(ggdf) + 
  geom_point(aes(x = value, y = froh, color = name)) + 
  geom_smooth(aes(x = value, y = froh, color = name), method = "lm") +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free") +
  theme_classic()
```

```{r}
# showing off bio2
het <- get_het()
coords <- left_join(coords, het)
dif_lh <- cur - lh
dif_vals <- extract(dif_lh, coords[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)
mod_df <- data.frame(coords, dif_vals)

gg_df <- mod_df

plt2 <-
ggplot(mod_df) +
  geom_sf(data = ca) +
  geom_point(aes(x = x, y = y, color = bio_2), cex = 2) +
  labs(color = "Change\nin mean\ndiurnal\nrange") +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "magma", direction = -1) +
  theme_void() 

plt3 <-
ggplot(gg_df) +
  geom_sf(data = ca) +
  geom_point(aes(x = x, y = y, color = Ho), cex = 2) +
  theme_void() +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "magma")
  

plt1 <- ggplot(gg_df, aes(bio_2, Ho)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  stat_regline_equation(
    aes(label =  paste(..eq.label.., ..adj.rr.label.., sep = "~~~~")),
    formula = y ~ x
  ) +
  xlab("Change in mean diurnal range") +
  stat_cor(label.y = 800000) +
  xlim(-7.5, NA) +
  theme_classic()

pdf(here("bio2.pdf"), width = 15, height = 5)
grid.arrange(plt1, plt2, plt3, nrow = 1)
dev.off()
```

### Evaluating variables correlated with bio2
```{r}
bio2_cors <- cor(mod_df %>% select(starts_with("bio")))[,"bio_2"]
bio2_cors <- names(bio2_cors[abs(bio2_cors) > 0.5])

#bio6 - min temperature of coldest month (bio6)
#bio5 - max temperature of warmest month (bio5)
#bio7 - temperature annual range (bio5-bio6)
#bio2 - mean diurnal range (mean of monthly (max temp - min temp)
#bio12 - annual precipitation
#bio13 - precipitation of wettest month

gg_df <- 
  mod_df %>% 
  pivot_longer(all_of(bio2_cors)) %>%
  # replace bio_2/bio_5/bio_7 with the actual variable name
  mutate(name = case_when(
    name == "bio_2" ~ "A. Change in mean diurnal range\n(BIO2)",
    name == "bio_5" ~ "B. Change in max temperature of warmest month\n(BIO5)",
    name == "bio_7" ~ "D. Change in temperature annual range\n(BIO7; BIO5 - BIO6)",
    name == "bio_6" ~ "C. Change in min temperature of coldest month\n(BIO6)",
    name == "bio_1" ~ "E. Change in mean annual temperature\n(BIO1)",
    name == "bio_12" ~ "F. Change in annual precipitation\n(BIO12)",
    name == "bio_13" ~ "G. Change in precipitation of wettest month\n(BIO13)",
    TRUE ~ name
  )) 

plt1 <- ggplot(gg_df, aes(value, Ho)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  stat_regline_equation(
    aes(label =  paste(..eq.label.., ..adj.rr.label.., sep = "~~~~")),
    formula = y ~ x
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free", nrow = 1) +
  stat_cor(label.y = 800000) +
  theme_classic() +
  xlab("value")

plt_list <- gg_df %>%
  split(.$name) %>%
  map(~ {
    limit <- max(abs(.x[["value"]])) * c(-1, 1)
    ggplot(.x) +
        geom_sf(data = ca, fill = "darkgray") +
        labs(color = "value") +
        geom_point(aes(x = x, y = y, color = value), cex = 2) +
        scale_color_distiller(type = "div", limit = limit) +
        facet_wrap(~name) +
        theme_void()}
        )

plt3 <-
ggplot(gg_df) +
  geom_sf(data = ca) +
  geom_point(aes(x = x, y = y, color = Ho)) +
  ggtitle("E. Genetic diversity") +
  theme_void() +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "magma")
  

ga1 <- do.call(grid.arrange, c(c(plt_list), nrow = 1))

grid.arrange(plt1, ga1, nrow =2)

summary(lm(Ho ~ bio_5 + bio_6, mod_df))
summary(lm(Ho ~ bio_2, mod_df))
```

### Testing for effects of population structure

```{r}
source(here("analysis", "admixture", "admixture.R"))
admix <- get_Q(K = 5)
head(admix)
coords <- left_join(coords, admix)
mod_df <- data.frame(coords, dif_vals)
# bio2 is still significant after controling for clusters
summary(lm(Ho ~ cluster + bio_2, data = mod_df))
# nothing is significant once an interaction term is added but I think that is due to low power
summary(lm(Ho ~ cluster + bio_2, data = mod_df))
mod_df %>% group_by(cluster) %>% summarise(mean = mean(Ho, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% arrange(desc(mean))

# when you remove the southern pop with really high genetic diversity (cluster 3), the pattern still exists (albeit weaker)
plt1 <- ggplot(mod_df[mod_df$cluster != 3, ], aes(bio_2, Ho)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  stat_regline_equation(
    aes(label =  paste(..eq.label.., ..adj.rr.label.., sep = "~~~~")),
    formula = y ~ x
  ) +
  stat_cor(label.y = 800000) +
  theme_classic()

plt2 <- ggplot(mod_df[mod_df$cluster != 3, ]) +
  geom_sf(data = ca) +
  geom_point(aes(x = x, y = y, color = bio_2), cex = 2) +
  labs(color = "bio_2") +
  theme_void() +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "magma", direction = -1)

plt3 <- ggplot(mod_df[mod_df$cluster != 3, ]) +
  geom_sf(data = ca) +
  geom_point(aes(x = x, y = y, color = Ho), cex = 2) +
  theme_void() +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "magma")

grid.arrange(plt1, plt2, plt3, nrow = 1)
```


# exploring further
```{r}
range_df <- 
  data.frame(
    bio_5_cur = extract(cur[["bio_5"]], coords[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE),
    bio_6_cur = extract(cur[["bio_6"]], coords[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE),
    bio_5_lh = extract(lh[["bio_5"]], coords[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE),
    bio_6_lh = extract(lh[["bio_6"]], coords[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE),
    Ho = coords$Ho
  )
names(range_df) <- c("bio_5_cur", "bio_6_cur", "bio_5_lh", "bio_6_lh", "Ho")

range_df <- 
  range_df %>% 
  pivot_longer(all_of(c("bio_5_cur", "bio_6_cur", "bio_5_lh", "bio_6_lh"))) %>%
  mutate(name = factor(name, levels = c("bio_5_cur", "bio_6_cur", "bio_5_lh", "bio_6_lh")))

ggplot(range_df) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(x = name, y = value))

```

```{r}
df <- coords
het <- get_het()
roh <- get_roh()
df <- left_join(df, het) %>% left_join(roh)
cur_vals <- extract(cur, df[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)
lh_vals <- extract(lh, df[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)
lgm_vals <- extract(lgm, df[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)
mh_vals <- extract(mh, df[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)
eh_vals <- extract(eh, df[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)
dif_lh <- cur - lh
dif_lh_vals <- extract(dif_lh, df[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)
dif_lgm <- cur - lgm
dif_lgm_vals <- extract(dif_lgm, df[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)
dif_mh <- cur - mh
dif_mh_vals <- extract(dif_mh, df[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)
dif_eh <- cur - eh
dif_eh_vals <- extract(dif_eh, df[,c("x", "y")], ID = FALSE)

cur_df <- data.frame(df, cur_vals)
lh_df <- data.frame(df, lh_vals)
lgm_df <- data.frame(df, lgm_vals)
mh_df <- data.frame(df, mh_vals)
eh_df <- data.frame(df, eh_vals)
dif_lh_df <- data.frame(df, dif_lh_vals)
dif_lgm_df <- data.frame(df, dif_lgm_vals)
dif_mh_df <- data.frame(df, dif_mh_vals)
dif_eh_df <- data.frame(df, dif_eh_vals)
#fHo <- formula(paste0("Ho ~", paste0(names(cur_vals), collapse = " + ")))
#summary(cor(fHo, cur_df))

# check for strong correlations

# only strong correlation is with the late holocene/bio2
map(list(cur_df, eh_df, mh_df, lh_df, lgm_df, dif_eh_df, dif_mh_df, dif_lh_df, dif_lgm_df), ~ {
  strong <- cor(.x %>% select(Ho, contains("bio")) %>% drop_na(Ho))["Ho", -1]
  which(abs(strong) > 0.4)
})

# confirmed no correlation with ROH:
map(list(cur_df, eh_df, mh_df, lh_df, lgm_df, dif_eh_df, dif_mh_df, dif_lh_df, dif_lgm_df), ~ {
  strong <- cor(.x %>% select(froh, contains("bio")) %>% drop_na(froh))["froh", -1]
  which(abs(strong) > 0.4)
})

summary(lm(froh ~ bio_9, dif_lh_df))

plot(dif_lh_df[["bio_9"]], dif_lh_df[["Ho"]])
```

```{r}

```
